---
title: "User Guide"
---

## Complete User Guide

Learn how to get the most out of Candidate Matcher.

---

## Getting Started

### First Time Use

1. **Open the app**: Navigate to your GitHub Pages URL
2. **Wait for data**: Jobs and candidates load automatically
3. **Check data status**: Top-right shows last refresh date

### Main Interface

The app has two main panels:

**Left Panel (Job Selection)**
- List of all active job postings
- Search and filter controls
- Multi-select checkboxes
- Selected jobs summary

**Right Panel (Candidate Results)**
- Matching candidates grid
- Filter controls (score, experience, search)
- Candidate cards with preview info

---

## Step-by-Step Workflow

### 1. Select Jobs

**Search for jobs:**
- Type in search box to filter by title, company, or location
- Use employment type dropdown for full-time/part-time/contract

**Select multiple jobs:**
- Click on job cards to select/deselect
- Hold Ctrl/Cmd to select multiple non-adjacent jobs
- Selected jobs show blue highlight

**Tip**: Select related jobs together (e.g., all "Software Engineer" roles) to find candidates matching any of them.

### 2. Find Matches

Click **"🔍 Find Matching Candidates"** button.

The system will:
- Score each candidate against selected jobs
- Pick the best matching job for each candidate
- Sort candidates by overall match score
- Display results in right panel

### 3. Filter Results

Use filters to narrow down:

**Search candidates:**
- Search by name, current title, or skills

**Minimum score:**
- 50%+: All reasonable matches
- 70%+: Strong matches only
- 85%+: Top candidates

**Experience level:**
- Filter by entry, mid, senior, or executive

### 4. Review Candidates

Click any candidate card to open detailed view.

**You'll see:**
- Full profile (name, title, company, location)
- Experience details (years, level, availability)
- Contact info (email, LinkedIn if available)
- Skills breakdown:
  - ✅ Matched skills (green)
  - ❌ Missing required skills (red)
  - Other skills (gray)
- Match score breakdown by category

### 5. Add Notes

In the candidate modal:

1. **Write your note** in the text area
   - Be specific about strengths/concerns
   - Include salary expectations, availability
   - Note any red flags or standout qualities

2. **Add rating** (optional)
   - 1-5 stars
   - Helps with sorting later

3. **Click "Save Note"**
   - Note is saved to browser localStorage
   - Appears in notes list immediately
   - Persists across sessions

**Tip**: Add notes right after reviewing - it's easy to forget details!

### 6. Export Results

When ready to share or backup:

1. Click **"📤 Export Results"** button
2. Choose format:
   - **CSV**: Opens in Excel/Google Sheets
   - **JSON**: For developers/data processing
3. File downloads automatically

**CSV includes:**
- All candidate fields
- Match scores
- All notes with timestamps
- Ratings

---

## Advanced Features

### Multi-Job Matching

Select multiple jobs to:
- Find candidates matching any of the selected roles
- Compare candidate fit across different levels
- Build pipeline for similar positions

**Example workflow:**
1. Select "Junior Developer", "Mid Developer", "Senior Developer"
2. See all candidates ranked by best fit
3. Note which level each candidate matches best

### Notes Management

**View all notes:**
- Click "📝 View All Notes" in header
- See notes from all candidates
- Sorted by newest first
- Statistics dashboard shows totals

**Export notes separately:**
- From notes page, click "📊 Export CSV"
- Get spreadsheet with all notes
- Filter/sort in Excel as needed

**Clear old notes:**
- Use "🗑️ Clear All Notes" (with confirmation)
- Export first as backup!

---

## Tips & Best Practices

### Efficient Review Process

1. **Batch by score**: Review 80%+ matches first
2. **Quick scan**: Skim skills preview before clicking
3. **Note immediately**: Don't move to next candidate without notes
4. **Use ratings**: 5-star system helps prioritize later
5. **Export weekly**: Backup notes before clearing cache

### Writing Good Notes

✅ **DO:**
- Be specific: "Strong Python, lacks AWS experience"
- Note concerns: "Salary expectation 20% above budget"
- Record availability: "Can start in 2 weeks"
- Culture fit: "Startup experience, may struggle in enterprise"
- Follow-up items: "Send coding challenge", "Schedule technical screen"

❌ **DON'T:**
- Vague statements: "Good candidate"
- Duplicate resume info without analysis
- Forget to save before closing modal!

### Filtering Strategies

**For urgent hires:**
- Min score: 85%+
- Availability: "immediate" or "2-weeks"
- Sort by score descending

**For pipeline building:**
- Min score: 50%+
- Broader net
- Tag with "future opportunity" notes

**For specific skill needs:**
- Search by skill name
- Check matched skills in modal
- Note training needs for near-matches

---

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `Ctrl+F` | Focus search box |
| `Esc` | Close modal |
| `Enter` | Confirm selection (in dialogs) |
| `Ctrl+S` | Save note (when in note editor) |

---

## Common Tasks

### "I need to find Python developers urgently"

1. Search jobs for "Python" or select relevant jobs
2. Set min score to 70%+
3. Search candidates for "Python"
4. Filter availability to "immediate"
5. Review top matches, add notes
6. Export CSV to share with hiring manager

### "I want to review all my notes from this week"

1. Click "📝 View All Notes"
2. Notes are sorted by date (newest first)
3. Export CSV if needed
4. Use browser's Find (Ctrl+F) to search text

### "How do I share candidates with my team?"

1. Select jobs and run matching
2. Add your notes/reviews
3. Click "📤 Export Results" → CSV
4. Email CSV to team
5. Or share GitHub Pages URL (they'll see data but not your notes)

### "I accidentally cleared my notes!"

If you exported recently:
1. Open the CSV in Excel
2. Manually re-add important notes
3. In future, export before clearing!

If no export: Unfortunately, localStorage clearing is permanent. Consider implementing Google Sheets sync for future.

---

## Troubleshooting

**No candidates showing after matching:**
- Lower minimum score filter
- Select different jobs
- Check if candidates table has data

**Notes not saving:**
- Ensure not in incognito/private mode
- Check browser storage settings
- Try different browser

**Match scores seem off:**
- Review candidate's full profile
- Check if required skills match
- Adjust weights in app.js if needed (advanced)

**Can't export:**
- Allow pop-ups for the site
- Check browser download settings
- Try different browser

---

## Next Steps

- [Matching Algorithm](matching-algorithm.qmd) - Understand scoring
- [Notes Feature](notes-feature.qmd) - Deep dive on notes
- [Documentation](documentation.qmd) - Full reference
- [FAQ](documentation.qmd#faq) - Common questions
